metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing note in the plan: removing `run_security_detectors` MUST atomically update `apply_failclosed` (detectors.py:106) to call `run_detectors` AND migrate all monkeypatches in test_code_review_ws4/ws5, test_det_invariants, test_review_bot, test_code_review_gate_session from `run_security_detectors` to `run_detectors`.
findings.finding: Removing `run_security_detectors` while `apply_failclosed` internally calls `run_security_detectors` (not `run_detectors`) creates an ordering/atomicity trap: the alias deletion must be coupled with updating line 106 of detectors.py to call `run_detectors` AND migrating all test monkeypatches from `run_security_detectors` to `run_detectors` — otherwise the test suite fails with a NameError or mocks the wrong target. The comment at detectors.py:104-105 explicitly documents this indirection as intentional; the plan's AC/Remediation do not mention this coupling.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation for `run_security_detectors`: 'Change apply_failclosed (detectors.py:106) to call run_detectors directly; update all monkeypatches in the five affected test files to target run_detectors instead of run_security_detectors in the same commit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before removing `run_security_detectors`, update `apply_failclosed` (detectors.py:106) to call `run_detectors` directly, and update all monkeypatch sites in tests (test_code_review_ws4.py, test_code_review_ws5.py, test_review_bot.py, test_code_review_gate_session.py, test_det_invariants.py) as named call sites.
findings.finding: `apply_failclosed` (detectors.py:106) deliberately calls `run_security_detectors` (not `run_detectors`) to support monkeypatching of either name — removing the alias without updating this internal self-call will silently break `apply_failclosed` at runtime. The plan's AC ('call sites and tests updated') does not name this site.
findings.location: Remediation / AC line 'Call sites and tests updated'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py:106
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation section add: 'Update `apply_failclosed` (detectors.py:106) to call `run_detectors` instead of `run_security_detectors` before the alias is deleted; update all `monkeypatch.setattr(_det, "run_security_detectors", ...)` test lines to target `run_detectors`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `apply_failclosed` in `detectors.py:106` deliberately calls `run_security_detectors` (not `run_detectors`) for monkeypatch-seam reasons before including it in the removal list; if the seam is needed, this alias cannot simply be dropped without refactoring the call site and test strategy.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `run_security_detectors` is a 'deprecated alias' safe to remove after confirming no live callers, but production code `apply_failclosed` in `detectors.py:106` calls `run_security_detectors` *intentionally* — the inline comment explicitly states this is required so that monkeypatching either name in the test suite works. Removal would silently break the monkeypatch-based test isolation strategy.
findings.suggested_fix: Before removing the alias, refactor `apply_failclosed` to call `run_detectors` directly and update all `monkeypatch.setattr(detectors, 'run_security_detectors', ...)` sites to patch `run_detectors` instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the ACTUAL code construct in `reducer/_cache.py` (or another file) that constitutes the claimed 'pre-v3/v4 replay-cache branch'; lines 13-24 are only comments + a constant, not a conditional branch.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `reducer/_cache.py:13-24` contains a 'pre-v3/v4 replay-cache branch' to remove, but those lines contain only version-history comments and the constant `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION = 4`. No conditional branch code exists at the cited location; there is nothing structurally removable there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `reducer/_cache.py:13-24` edit target: lines 13-24 are a version-history comment block, not an executable branch — specify whether the intent is to delete the comment block, bump `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION`, or something else.
findings.finding: The plan's `reducer/_cache.py:13-24` 'pre-v3/v4 replay-cache branch' edit target does not correspond to any conditional branching code — lines 13-24 are a comment block documenting version history; the version is folded into the dir-hash string at line 59 (`hash_parts = [f'rv:{_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION}']`), which causes old caches to auto-invalidate without a runtime branch. There is no executable 'pre-v3/v4 branch' to remove.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify exactly what code in `reducer/_cache.py` is targeted for removal: if it is the `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` constant and associated comment history, say so explicitly; if it is something else (e.g. a conditional branch in a caller), cite the specific lines to be removed.
findings.finding: The plan targets removal of the `reducer/_cache.py` 'pre-v3/v4 replay-cache branch', but the visible code has no such conditional branch — `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION = 4` is folded into the dir-hash string in `compute_dir_hash`, making old caches auto-invalidate by hash mismatch, with no separate code path handling the old format. There is no dead branch to remove; the plan may be misidentifying the version constant itself or a non-existent conditional as the removal target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that `NextBatchOut`, `ValidateReportOut`, and `ClarityResultOut` are currently imported from `rebar.mcp_server` in tests (not `rebar._mcp_models`), and add an explicit step to migrate those import sites before removing the re-export shim.
findings.finding: The plan states that the `_mcp_models` re-export shim in `mcp_server.py:20,34` should be removed after verifying 'no external importer', but the existing test suite already imports `NextBatchOut`, `ValidateReportOut`, and `ClarityResultOut` directly from `rebar.mcp_server` — the shim has confirmed live callers the plan's precondition fails against.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a migration sub-step: update `test_next_batch_compute.py:406`, `test_validate_compute.py:138`, `test_library.py:66` (and any others found by grep) to import from `rebar._mcp_models` directly, then remove the re-exports from `mcp_server.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all files outside `src/rebar/` that import symbols from `rebar.mcp_server` (at minimum: `tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py`, `test_validate_compute.py`, `tests/interfaces/facades/test_library.py`, `test_workflow_mcp_schema.py`, `scripts/check_server_manifest.py`, and ~15 others) as explicit migration targets in the AC, since the 'verify no external importer' precondition is already falsified.
findings.finding: The plan names `_mcp_models` re-export shim removal at `mcp_server.py:20,34` with 'verify no external importer' as a precondition, but at least 20 test files outside `src/rebar/` already import model symbols (NextBatchOut, ValidateReportOut, ClarityResultOut, BridgeFsckOut, etc.) directly from `rebar.mcp_server` — these are confirmed external consumers that the plan does not enumerate.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC precondition 'confirm _mcp_models … has no external importers' with 'migrate all external importers of mcp_server model symbols to import from rebar._mcp_models directly, then remove the re-export block' — and list the ~20 affected files explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the open 'verify no external importer' note for `_mcp_models` with a concrete list of the three known test-layer importers (test_next_batch_compute.py, test_validate_compute.py, test_library.py) that must be updated to import from `rebar._mcp_models` directly when the re-export shim is removed.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'verify no external importer' for `_mcp_models` re-exports in mcp_server.py, but the codebase already has known importers: tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py imports `NextBatchOut`, tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py imports `ValidateReportOut`, and tests/interfaces/facades/test_library.py imports `ClarityResultOut` — all from `rebar.mcp_server`. Treating verification as a future open question understates the known update surface and risks the implementer removing the re-exports without updating these imports.
findings.location: Remediation / cascade_flag — 'verify `_mcp_models` and `run_security_detectors` have no external importers'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation: 'Known importers of the mcp_server re-exports: test_next_batch_compute.py (NextBatchOut), test_validate_compute.py (ValidateReportOut), test_library.py (ClarityResultOut) — update each to import from rebar._mcp_models directly.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision rule to the Remediation for the `_mcp_models` shim: since test files import model classes from `rebar.mcp_server`, specify whether (a) those test imports should be redirected to `rebar._mcp_models` before the shim is removed, or (b) the re-exports in `mcp_server.__all__` are retained as a stable public surface (not a shim to remove).
findings.finding: The plan's cascade_flag says 'confirm `_mcp_models` and `run_security_detectors` have no external importers' before removing the `mcp_server.py` re-export shim, but grep confirms tests actively import `NextBatchOut`, `ValidateReportOut`, and `ClarityResultOut` from `rebar.mcp_server` — exactly the symbols the shim re-exports. The plan provides no decision rule for what to do when such importers ARE found (update imports, keep shim, or both), leaving the executor without a processing protocol for the discovered condition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `langfuse_cfg` removal at `prompts.py:627` targets only the private helper function (not `resolve_prompt` at line 385 whose `langfuse_cfg` is a documented, CI-enforced public param), and confirm `docs/reuse-surface.md` and `test_reuse_surface_doc.py` require no change.
findings.finding: The plan targets `prompts.py:627` `langfuse_cfg` param removal, but `langfuse_cfg` also appears in `resolve_prompt` at `prompts.py:385` (a live public signature), `docs/reuse-surface.md:256` (documented API), and `test_reuse_surface_doc.py:44` (CI-enforced signature assertion). These are unenumerated consumers outside `src/rebar/llm/prompting/`; removing the param from the `:627` helper while keeping it in `resolve_prompt`'s signature is the likely intent, but this is not stated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the 5 test files that reference `run_security_detectors` (`test_code_review_ws4.py`, `test_code_review_ws5.py`, `test_review_bot.py`, `test_code_review_gate_session.py`, `test_det_invariants.py`) as explicit migration targets in the AC for the `detectors.py` alias removal.
findings.finding: The `run_security_detectors` deprecated alias at `detectors.py:79-83` has active consumers outside `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` — specifically `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws4.py`, `test_code_review_ws5.py`, `test_review_bot.py`, `test_code_review_gate_session.py`, and `test_det_invariants.py` — none of which are enumerated in the plan as migration targets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a discrete AC entry requiring an explicit grep/audit result confirming zero external importers for `_mcp_models` (mcp_server.py) and `run_security_detectors` (detectors.py) before those two are removed.
findings.finding: The two higher-risk removals — `_mcp_models` re-export and `run_security_detectors` alias — carry a specific caller-verification requirement ('verify no external importer') that appears only in prose/cascade_flag, not in the Acceptance Criteria. This means there is no end-state-observable proof for these items in the ACs; a reviewer or implementer could close the ticket with the test suite green without having performed the external-importer audit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 1 ('Each listed shim/param removed after confirming internal-only usage') and cascade_flag prose
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Grep/audit confirms no external (outside the repo) importers of `_mcp_models` and `run_security_detectors`; result documented in PR description.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-removal step specifying how existing on-disk pre-v3/v4 cache files are handled at cutover (e.g., delete/invalidate them before branch removal, or confirm the reader fails-open and regenerates cleanly on any unrecognised format, with a test covering that path).
findings.finding: The plan removes the pre-v3/v4 branch from `reducer/_cache.py` without specifying what happens to existing on-disk cache files that were written in the old (pre-v3/v4) format: whether old-format files are silently skipped, cause a read error, or are transparently regenerated. If a running instance has old-format cache files present at the moment of deployment, the removal of the branch could cause the cache reader to error or silently corrupt its regenerated output rather than cleanly fall through.
findings.suggested_fix: Before removing the branch, add a one-time cache-invalidation step (delete or rename the cache directory/files) as part of the deployment runbook, OR confirm in the plan that the reader already fails-open (catches unknown format → regenerates) and add a regression test for that behaviour so the 'cache still regenerates correctly' assertion is mechanically verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assert in the plan that `RunRequest` is never serialised to any durable store, queue, or replay log (or, if it is, describe the dual-write/migration window and confirm old payloads are drained before the field is removed).
findings.finding: The plan removes a historical structured-output strategy field from the RunRequest contract (`runner.py:67-70`) but does not address whether RunRequest objects are ever serialised to a queue, database, or durable store (e.g., a job queue, message broker, or replay log). If any RunRequest is serialised and an in-flight request carries the now-removed field, deserialisation after the field is dropped could silently discard or fail on that field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the fallback decision rule for items where an external caller IS found (e.g., 'skip removal, open a follow-up ticket, and note in PR') so the executor need not invent policy.
findings.finding: The plan conditions removal of `_mcp_models` (and implicitly all items) on 'confirming internal-only usage' but states no decision rule for the case where an external importer IS found — leaving the executor to invent the fallback policy.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'If an external importer is discovered for any listed item, skip that removal, document the finding in the PR description, and open a follow-up ticket — do not block the rest of the sweep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3 (e.g., `pytest tests/reducer/test_cache.py -k regen` or a short script that deletes the cache and confirms it is rebuilt) and for AC4 (e.g., `grep -rn 'event_schema\|on_disk' src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` returns no deletions, or a dedicated replay-compat test run).
findings.finding: AC3 ('`reducer/_cache.py` pre-v3/v4 branch removed (cache still regenerates correctly)') and AC4 ('No change to event-schema / on-disk-store replay compat') each lack a concrete proving command; 'correctly' is a hedge and the negative AC4 claim is unmeasured.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC3 with a specific observable: e.g., 'delete the cache dir, run the reducer, assert the cache directory is recreated and contains valid entries (validated by the existing cache-hit test)'.
findings.finding: AC3's outcome 'cache still regenerates correctly' is a subjective/vague observable — 'correctly' names no specific, measurable system behaviour (what output, what file, what assertion).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., revert commit, or expand-contract phasing) for the removal batch, and define the go/no-go decision if the `_mcp_models` or `run_security_detectors` external-importer scan returns a hit.
findings.finding: The plan removes multiple internal-only interfaces and branches (8 items) but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if removal breaks something in CI, a downstream internal consumer not caught by grep, or if the `_mcp_models`/`run_security_detectors` external-importer verification fails. T4 requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of internal-only scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If any removal is found to have an external caller post-merge, revert the relevant commit and open a follow-up ticket to apply expand-contract (deprecation warning first, removal next release). If external-importer scan for `_mcp_models` or `run_security_detectors` returns a hit before merge, do NOT remove — instead treat as an external-contract item and carry forward to the post-beta deprecation sweep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Locate the actual `_detector_matches` / `detector_matches` re-export shim (if any) that SC2 is supposed to handle — it does not exist at `llm/plan_review/registry.py:55`; verify the scope exclusion references a real code entity or remove the exclusion.
findings.finding: The scope-exclusion claim cites '`llm/plan_review/registry.py:55` `_detector_matches` re-export' as handled by an SC2 ticket, but no `_detector_matches` symbol exists anywhere in `plan_review/registry.py`; line 55 contains a comment about `RegistryError`. The scoped-out entity is unverifiable and references a non-existent code location.
findings.location: Finding section (preamble) — '(Excludes `llm/plan_review/registry.py:55` `_detector_matches` re-export, which is handled by the SC2 ticket.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the `acli_cmd` removal scope to `transition_issue` in `acli.py` only; explicitly exclude `acli_subprocess._run_acli`, `AcliClient.__init__`, and `acli_cli_ops.*` where `acli_cmd` is functionally live and used for test injection.
findings.finding: The plan names `acli.py:146` `acli_cmd` param as 'no longer used', but the parameter is live and functionally used throughout `acli_cli_ops.py` (15+ sites) and `acli_subprocess._run_acli` (line 307: `base = acli_cmd if acli_cmd is not None else _DEFAULT_ACLI_CMD`). Tests in `tests/scripts/conftest.py` and `tests/scripts/bridge/test_bridge_acli_field_coverage.py` pass `acli_cmd=` to these live functions. Only `transition_issue` in `acli.py` (line 126) ignores its `acli_cmd` param; all other functions in the module tree actively use it.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan bullet from '`acli.py:146` `acli_cmd` param' to '`acli.py:146` `transition_issue` — drop its dead `acli_cmd` keyword parameter only; do NOT touch `acli_subprocess._run_acli`, `acli_cli_ops.*`, or `AcliClient.__init__` where the param is live.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'handled by the SC2 ticket' with the actual ticket ID (or a verifiable link) so an executor can confirm the carve-out is intentional and the sibling work is tracked.
findings.finding: The plan carves out `llm/plan_review/registry.py:55` `_detector_matches` re-export as 'handled by the SC2 ticket' but does not provide the SC2 ticket ID or a link, making the exclusion boundary unverifiable by an executor performing the sweep — they cannot confirm coverage without finding an unnamed ticket.
findings.location: Finding section: '(Excludes `llm/plan_review/registry.py:55` `_detector_matches` re-export, which is handled by the SC2 ticket.)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
